description: Execute a plan until it is complete

steps.label: Planner Select And Store Next Plan

content: Use fresh disk reads and current git state, not conversational memory. Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json`, and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Reopen the exact relative `plan_path` from disk even if you have read it before, using explicit shell reads such as `sed`, `cat`, or `rg`. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. Recheck the current repository branch directly from git, for example with `git branch --show-current`, and recheck the most recent commit directly from git, for example with `git log --oneline -1`. If either of those scope checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. Recheck each additional repository branch directly from git, for example with `git -C <repo_root> branch --show-current`, and recheck its most recent commit directly from git, for example with `git -C <repo_root> log --oneline -1`. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. You MUST double-check whether tasks have been added to the plan by another agent or whether anything has changed. You must ensure you fully understand the plan and its remaining tasks before proceeding. You MUST read the whole plan again and CANNOT assume you understand it from before. Once you have done this, give me a summary of the remaining work.

Reconfirm repository-defined manual testing skip policy

content: Before manual-testing work continues, re-check `AGENTS.md` and `codeinfo_markdown/repository_information.md` for any repository-defined conditions that allow manual proof to be narrowed or skipped. Follow those repository-owned rules if they are being met during proof. Do not invent your own skip reasons, and do not reopen the task or add implementation work solely because a repository-defined skip condition was hit.

steps.label: Ensure Unresolved Review Findings Became Tasks
Refresh Current Plan Handoff After Review Task-Up
Ensure Review-Created Testing Steps Are Correct
Exit Review-Created Task Scope Loop If Context Is Not Safely Usable
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json`, and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this step. Then re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk using explicit shell reads such as `sed`, `cat`, or `rg`. Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` from disk after `current-plan.json` when it exists and is valid enough to supply current review-cycle context, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json`. Derive the story number from `plan_path`, then read `codeInfoTmp/reviews/<story-number>-current-review.json` from disk, for example with `cat codeInfoTmp/reviews/<story-number>-current-review.json`, whenever the current review handoff is needed to identify the active review-created block safely. Do not rely on conversational memory, prior loop passes, earlier summaries, or any previous snapshot. Return exact JSON only: {"answer":"yes"} or {"answer":"no"}. Answer `yes` if this loop should exit cleanly without another repair pass because the context is not safely usable, including when `current-plan.json` is missing, unreadable, malformed, or does not name a usable canonical `plan_path`; when the canonical plan is missing, unreadable, or unusable; when the current branch story number does not match the selected plan filename; when both the review disposition state and the current review handoff are missing, unreadable, malformed, or otherwise unusable for active review-cycle identification; when multiple plausible current-cycle review-created blocks still remain after the selector order; or when the active review-created block still cannot be identified safely. Answer `no` only when the loop context is safe enough to continue into the repair step.

steps.label: Exit Review-Created Task Scope Loop When Clean
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json`, and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this step. Then read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` from disk after `current-plan.json` when it exists and is valid enough to provide the active review-cycle context, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json`. Re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk immediately before answering, using explicit shell reads such as `sed`, `cat`, or `rg`. Do not rely on conversational memory, prior loop passes, earlier summaries, or any previous snapshot because the plan may have been modified by another llm in the previous loop pass. Inspect only the newly added or updated review-created tasks for the current review cycle. Return exact JSON only: {"answer":"yes"} or {"answer":"no"}. Answer `yes` only if every newly added or updated review-created task is now fully within current-story scope across all relevant sections, including heading/title, `Repository Name`, `Task Dependencies`, `Notes`, `Overview`, `Task Exit Criteria`, `Addresses Findings`, `Risk Ownership`, `Owner Map`, `Requirement-To-Proof Mapping` or `Proof Mapping`, `Affected Repositories`, `Documentation Locations`, `Subtasks`, `Testing`, `Manual Testing Guidance`, and `Implementation Notes`. Answer `no` if another repair pass is still needed.
